P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
•• 
Administration

(916) 322·5662 
Councilman Ed Struiksma 
San Diego City Council 
202 C Street 
San Diego, CA 
92101 
322·5660 
May 30, 1984 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6""1 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-l29 
Dear Councilman Struiksma: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Commissioner Stanford 
asked me to respond to your question. 
FACTS 
On May 3, 1984, you attended a charity fundraiser for the 
Y.M.C.A. as the guest of a business entity. 
Everyone who 
attended the event received a ticket for a drawing. 
Your ticket 
was drawn and you received a $250 Small Savers Certificate 
issued by the National Bank of La Jolla. 
You are going to place 
the certificate in the name of one of your children. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
How should the Small Savers Certificate be reported on your 
Statement of Economic Interests? 
DISCUSSION 
You should report the $250 Small Savers Certificate on your 
1984 Statement of Economic Interests (Schedule F-Giftsl as a 
gift from the Y.M.C.A. 
(Government Code Section 82028_/ and 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section l8728(d), copy enclosed.) 
This 
reporting requirement exists, despite the fact that you are 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 


Councilman Ed Struiksma 
May 30, 1984 
Page 2 
placing the Certificate in your child's name, because, during 
the reporting period, you exercised direction and control over 
the account. 
In the future, neither the account, nor any 
interest generated by it, will be reportable. 
For the 12 months 
following the receipt of the Certificate, you will be subject to 
possible disqualification on decisions affecting the Y.M.C.A. 
(Sections 87100 and 87103). 
If I can be of any additional help to you, please feel free 
to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours,

Jan1S Shank McLean

THE CITY OF 
SAN DIEGO 
ED STRUIKSMA 
COUNCIL-MAN 
May 15, 1984 
Ms. Barbara Milman 
General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 "K" Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
I am requesting your opinion on the 
proper reporting practice I should follow 
based upon the following: 
On May 3rd of this year I was the guest 
of a company at a charity function for the 
Y.M.C.A. 
Everyone who attended received a 
ticket that went into a drum for a later 
drawing. 
During the course of the evening, a 
number of lucky tickets were drawn, and I was 
the holder of such a ticket. 
My door prize 
was a $250 Small Savers Certificate, issued 
by the National Bank of La Jolla (see 
attached). 
I will be placing the name of one 
of my children on this account, but I don't 
know if that has any affect on how this item 
should be reported. 
I appreciate your time and your opinion, 
and should you need any additional informa-
tion, please calIon me. 
SiI)cer~ly, 
ED STRUIKSMA 
Councilman 
District Five 
City Administration Building • Charles C. Dail Concourse 
• 202 C Street • San Diego, Califonia 92101 • Phone 236-6655 


NAT10Nf1L Bf\NJ~ 
OF;;' 
Lf\ 
JOLLA 
200 FOR YOUTH DINNER 
May 3, 1984 
CONGRATULATIONS! 
You are the lucky recipient of a $250.00 Small Savers Certificate 
issued by the National Bank of La Jolla. 
This certificate, which 
carries a current interest rate of 9.50% and a maturity of two 
and one-half years, is being held for you at the Bank's La Jolla 
Office located at 7660 Fay A venue. 
Please present this letter with proper identification to Lucy Jackson, 
Vice President and Client Service Manager, and Mrs. Jackson will 
issue the certificate in your name or any other individual designated 
by you. 
Again, congratulations, and thank you for your support of the 
Northwest YMCA 200 For Youth Dinner! 
Michael K. West 
President 
P.O. Box 2346, La Jolla, California 92038. (619) 454-9535

sections.question:
How should the Small Savers Certificate be reported on your 
Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion: You should report the $250 Small Savers Certificate on your 1984 Statement of Economic Interests (Schedule F-Gifts) as a gift from the Y.M.C.A. (Government Code Section 82028 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18728(d)). This reporting requirement exists, despite the fact that you are placing the Certificate in your child's name, because, during the reporting period, you exercised direction and control over the account. In the future, neither the account, nor any interest generated by it, will be reportable. For the 12 months following the receipt of the Certificate, you will be subject to possible disqualification on decisions affecting the Y.M.C.A. (Sections 87100 and 87103).

the certificate in the name of one of your children.

sections.question_synthetic: A councilman received a $250 Small Savers Certificate as a prize at a charity fundraiser and plans to place it in his child's name; he seeks guidance on how to report this on his Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The councilman must report the $250 certificate as a gift from the Y.M.C.A. on Schedule F of his Statement of Economic Interests because he exercised direction and control over it during the reporting period, regardless of later placing it in his child's name. After the initial reporting, the account and interest are no longer reportable, but he faces potential disqualification from Y.M.C.A.-related decisions for 12 months.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated May 15, 1984) and FPPC response letter (dated May 30, 1984). All key sections clearly identifiable. Supporting documentation (bank certificate letter) included. High confidence in extraction of question and conclusion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should the Small Savers Certificate be reported on your 
Statement of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION: You should report the $250 Small Savers Certificate on your 1984 Statement of Economic Interests (Schedule F-Gifts) as a gift from the Y.M.C.A. (Government Code Section 82028 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18728(d)). This reporting requirement exists, despite the fact that you are placing the Certificate in your child's name, because, during the reporting period, you exercised direction and control over the account. In the future, neither the account, nor any interest generated by it, will be reportable. For the 12 months following the receipt of the Certificate, you will be subject to possible disqualification on decisions affecting the Y.M.C.A. (Sections 87100 and 87103).

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-129 responds to Councilman Ed Struiksma's inquiry about reporting a $250 Small Savers Certificate won at a Y.M.C.A. charity fundraiser, advising that it must be reported as a gift on Schedule F despite his plan to place it in his child's name. The letter clarifies the reporting requirements under Government Code Section 82028 and explains the 12-month disqualification provisions.
